message:
fix(choreography): parallel knowledge-of-choice validation, compensation stubs, LabRuntime tests

mod.rs: Add first_active_participants() for parallel composition

The existing first_active_participant() returns a single participant, which
is correct for sequential composition but wrong for Par nodes where either
branch can progress first. The new method collects all possible first-active
participants across parallel branches via collect_first_active_participants().

The knowledge-of-choice validation (validate_knowledge_of_choice) now uses
this set-based approach: for each first sender in a branch, if it differs
from the decider, a KnowledgeOfChoice violation is reported. Previously,
Par branches where one side started with the decider and the other started
with a different participant would incorrectly pass validation.

Test: knowledge_of_choice_violation_detected_when_parallel_branch_starts_elsewhere
constructs a Choice where the then-branch is Par(a→b, c→d) with decider=a.
Even though a sends first in the left branch, c sends first in the right
branch, which must be flagged as a violation.

codegen.rs: Render compensation handler bodies as commented-out code

Previously, compensation handlers in generated code contained live
statements (e.g., CompensateMsg sends). This is dangerous because the
generated code is a scaffold — executing compensation actions before the
developer has reviewed them could cause unintended side effects.

Now, render_handler_body output for compensate blocks is line-by-line
prefixed with '//' to produce a commented-out skeleton that developers
must explicitly uncomment after review.

Test: generated_code_compensation_actions_are_stubbed verifies that
CompensateMsg sends appear only as comments in generated output.

pipeline.rs: Migrate lab test scaffold from tokio::spawn to LabRuntime

Lab tests previously used #[tokio::test] async fn with tokio::spawn for
participant tasks. This is now migrated to synchronous #[test] fn with
LabRuntime::new() and closure-based participant handles (renamed from
{name}_handle to _{name}_task). This aligns with the deterministic
LabRuntime testing infrastructure used elsewhere in the codebase.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
